message: Close H1 packs and verify H2.1 single-writer hot paths

 {"id":"bd-db300.7.9.4","title":"G9.4: reconcile stale placeholder gates and older contention assumptions with the real harnesses","description":"Reconcile the stale placeholder contention gates and earlier assumptions with the actual 2026-03-23 harnesses, artifacts, and failure modes.

This bead explicitly updates the project’s truth surface so old narrative residue stops steering priority.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:27:03.593921457Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-24T18:16:44.458725610Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-g","governance","overlay-2026-03-23","reality-check","track-g"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:28.096257070Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:27:03.593921457Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:43:30.131413373Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2554,"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.4","author":"codex","text":"## Addendum: reconciliation after gate update

This bead now depends on `G9.1` so that stale placeholder and legacy contention assumptions are reconciled against the updated executable gate surface, not against a half-defined intermediate state.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:43:30Z"},{"id":2584,"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.4","author":"codex","text":"## Addendum: stale placeholder gate specifics

This bead should explicitly reconcile the known placeholder surface in `crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/bd_3wop3_7_contention_elimination.rs` with the actual 2026-03-23 harnesses and artifacts. The older path reportedly still describes the FrankenSQLite throughput path as effectively sequential, which makes it unfit as a serious regression gate for the concurrent-writer thesis.

Closing this bead should therefore mean more than updating prose. It should leave the project with executable gates and harness assumptions that reflect the real c1 and persistent `8t/16t` benchmark story instead of outdated contention folklore.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:53:34Z"},{"id":2691,"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.4","author":"codex","text":"Stale placeholder contention assumptions are now retired more aggressively at the source surface.

What changed:
- `crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/bd_3wop3_7_contention_elimination.rs` now points operators directly to `scripts/capture_c1_evidence_pack.sh` and `scripts/capture_persistent_phase_pack.sh` in the file header, stale gate panics, and manual scaling-report note.
- The persistent scorecard now publishes the measured culprit surface instead of leaving older contention folklore unchallenged.

Current measured evidence wired into the truth surface:
- `persistent_concurrent_write_8t`: still `red` even though timeout wakes median is `0`; p95 latency is `28.0x` same-pack `sqlite3`, and `lock_topology_limited` stayed false in all captured MVCC samples.
- `persistent_concurrent_write_16t`: `red` with timeout wakes median `3939`, takeover median `1296`, `wal_append p99` median `848896us`, `phase_B p99` median `1036445us`, and `lock_topology_limited` again false in all captured MVCC samples.
- That means the old “serialized contention placeholder” story is no longer defensible as the primary explanatory surface for the current 16t collapse.

Verification:
- `bash -n scripts/capture_c1_evidence_pack.sh scripts/capture_persistent_phase_pack.sh` -> pass
- `RENDER_ONLY=1 ./scripts/capture_persistent_phase_pack.sh artifacts/persistent_phase_pack_20260323_222108` -> pass
- `jq '.honest_gate_summary,.critical_regimes[0],.critical_regimes[1]' artifacts/persistent_phase_pack_20260323_222108/persistent_scorecard.json` -> pass
- `git diff --check` -> pass

Residual risk:
- The placeholder test file is now clearly non-authoritative, but it still exists and still contains historical constants. That is acceptable for now because the comments/panics now redirect operators to the real pack surfaces, but final cleanup may still want a stronger separation between historical scaffolding and live gates.
","created_at":"2026-03-24T18:16:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","title":"G9.5: publish the final overlay scorecard and closeout decision record","description":"Publish the final overlay scorecard and closeout decision record once the evidence, interventions, and governance updates settle.

This should leave the graph in a state where a future agent can tell what changed, what remains open, and what to do next without reopening old debate.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:27:04.212302540Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-24T20:43:45.148371789Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-g","evidence","overlay-2026-03-23","report","scorecard","track-g","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:09.295220391Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:32.845825051Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:27:04.212302540Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:30.088207501Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:30.759012032Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:31.469362339Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:32.127982733Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hjkbr.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T20:43:45.147194344Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2581,"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","author":"codex","text":"## Suggested acceptance shape

The closeout here should point future users to the runnable gate entrypoints, the final evidence packs, the approved advanced frontier, and any still-red regimes. A strong result should still be humble enough to say what remains bad or uncertain.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:47:15Z"},{"id":2594,"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","author":"codex","text":"## Addendum: explicit final verdict semantics

The final scorecard should state, in plain terms, whether each critical benchmark regime cleared both parity and the program's healthy-margin standard. It should not force future users to infer the answer from geomeans or prose.

At minimum the closeout should call out:
- which critical c1 cells are above or below `1.0x`,
- which persistent `2t/4t/8t/16t` regimes are above or below `1.0x`,
- which cells cleared the healthy-margin threshold versus merely scraping above parity,
- what remains red, uncertain, or rolled back.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:56:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.8","title":"Track H: single-writer containment and honest comparison-mode cleanup","description":"## Goal
This track isolates and repairs the extra pain in forced single-writer mode without ever making it the product default. Single-writer mode is still important as a comparison path and fallback, but current c4/c8 numbers show that it is suffering from avoidable overhead on top of unavoidable serialization. Cleaning that up makes comparisons fairer and prevents shared fixes from accidentally helping only MVCC.

## Acceptance Criteria
- There is an evidence-backed explanation for why single-writer mode is still slow.
- Avoidable single-writer-only overhead is reduced.
- The final scorecard reports the mode honestly without confusing it for the project's main value proposition.
- This bead set explicitly includes comprehensive unit-test, end-to-end, and structured-logging obligations rather than treating verification as implied follow-up work.","design":"- Separate unavoidable serialization from self-inflicted waiting and duplicate work.
- Reuse the same hot-path optimizations wherever possible so improvements benefit both modes.
- Keep the purpose of the mode explicit: comparison/fallback, not the project's default operating model.","acceptance_criteria":"- There is an evidence-backed explanation for why single-writer mode is still slow.
- Avoidable single-writer-only overhead is reduced.
- The final scorecard reports the mode honestly without confusing it for the project's main value proposition.
- This bead set explicitly includes comprehensive unit-test, end-to-end, and structured-logging obligations rather than treating verification as implied follow-up work.","notes":"Current corrected averages show the scale of the problem: c4 mixed_read_write SQLite 2769.5 vs MVCC 424.2 vs single-writer 130.9; c8 mixed_read_write SQLite 965.3 vs MVCC 620.1 vs single-writer 80.8. Some of this is inherent serialization, but not all of it.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:47:39.567907034Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:17.975105757Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","busy","db300-program","db300-track-h","evidence","legacy","locking","perf","sqlite","track-h"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2263,"issue_id":"bd-db300.8","author":"codex","text":"Single-writer mode exists to provide an honest reference path and fallback, not to redefine the product. The purpose of this track is to strip away avoidable overhead so comparisons against SQLite are real, while preserving the non-negotiable rule that concurrent-writer mode remains the default and the point of the project.","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:48:00Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.8.1","title":"H1: profile the slowdown unique to forced single-writer mode","description":"## Goal
Profile the extra slowdown unique to forced single-writer mode under the hot cells and placement profiles that matter so the project can separate inherent serialization cost from self-inflicted overhead and topology-hostile waiting. Right now the mode is so much worse than MVCC that it clearly deserves a targeted breakdown instead of hand-waving.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. The dominant single-writer-only costs are identified with evidence on the same canonical hot cells and placement profiles used for SQLite and MVCC comparison.
2. The report distinguishes unavoidable serialization from avoidable waits, wake storms, cache-line ownership churn, or duplicate work.
3. The output includes a side-by-side artifact pack for the matched SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer runs so H2 can target only the truly single-writer-specific pain.
4. The findings directly motivate H2 and H3.
5. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"1.  {"id":"bd-db300.8.1.1","title":"H1.1: collect matched artifact packs across modes and placement profiles","description":"## Goal
collect matched artifact packs across modes and placement profiles

## Checklist
1. Collect matched SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer artifact packs on the same hot cells and placement profiles.
2. Keep the packs mechanically comparable.
3. Tie them back to canonical matrix rows and provenance.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Collect matched SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer artifact packs on the same hot cells and placement profiles.
2. Keep the packs mechanically comparable.
3. Tie them back to canonical matrix rows and provenance.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Matched SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer artifact packs are collected on the same canonical rows and placement profiles, with identical row identity and provenance fields.
2. The packs are mechanically comparable without hand-normalization and include enough evidence to isolate what slowdown is unique to forced single-writer mode versus shared baseline tax.
3. Artifact locations, bundle names, and rerun entrypoints follow the canonical naming/provenance contract so future operators can reproduce and compare them quickly.
4. The resulting pack is strong enough to support later single-writer diagnosis and honest scorecard reporting, not just ad hoc debugging.","notes":"Matched-pack obligations:
- collect side-by-side SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer packs for the same hot rows, profiles, binaries, and run identity fields
- require the bundle to contain enough counters/logs/artifacts to separate unavoidable serialization from extra self-inflicted overhead in single-writer mode
- align naming, retention, and provenance with A2.4 so later diagnosis and reporting do not depend on manual artifact archaeology
- optimize for users by giving them one directly comparable evidence pack instead of three separately interpreted runs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:28:43.854715489Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-15T08:59:08Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T08:59:08Z","close_reason":"Collected six matched baseline-unpinned SQLite/MVCC/single-writer artifact packs via scripts/verify_bd_db300_8_1_1_matched_artifact_packs.sh; report at artifacts/perf/bd-db300.8.1.1/bd-db300.8.1.1-20260415T085024Z-1432166/report.json.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-h","evidence","legacy","track-h"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:25:32.763073370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-db300.8.2","title":"H2: remove single-writer-only wait amplification and duplicate serialized work","description":"## Goal
Remove single-writer-specific wait amplification and duplicated serialized work that makes the fallback or reference path far worse than unavoidable serialization alone would suggest. The target is not to make single-writer mode the star, but to stop it from distorting comparisons through topology-blind baton passing, generic waits, or duplicated work inside the serialized region.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. At least one avoidable single-writer-only source of waiting, wake amplification, or duplicate work is removed.
2. Hot-cell benchmarks show the mode improved on its own terms across the same placement profiles used in H1.
3. The cleanup does not change the project's MVCC-by-default behavior.
4. Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
5. Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","acceptance_criteria":"1. At least one avoidable single-writer-only source of waiting, wake amplification, or duplicate work is removed.
2. Hot-cell benchmarks show the mode improved on its own terms across the same placement profiles used in H1.
3. The cleanup does not change the project's MVCC-by-default behavior.
4. Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
5. Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","notes":"This bead is likely to intersect both retry policy and commit-path scope. It should lean on B1's lock map rather than rediscovering the same synchronization topology from scratch, and it should be careful not to turn single-writer cleanup into a justification for weakening MVCC.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:18:06.438428589Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:18.376498375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["busy","critical-path","db300-program","db300-track-h","legacy","locking","perf","sqlite","track-h"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-db300.8.2","title":"H2: remove single-writer-only wait amplification and duplicate serialized work","description":"## Goal
Remove single-writer-specific wait amplification and duplicated serialized work that makes the fallback or reference path far worse than unavoidable serialization alone would suggest. The target is not to make single-writer mode the star, but to stop it from distorting comparisons through topology-blind baton passing, generic waits, or duplicated work inside the serialized region.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. At least one avoidable single-writer-only source of waiting, wake amplification, or duplicate work is removed.
2. Hot-cell benchmarks show the mode improved on its own terms across the same placement profiles used in H1.
3. The cleanup does not change the project's MVCC-by-default behavior.
4. Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
5. Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","acceptance_criteria":"1. At least one avoidable single-writer-only source of waiting, wake amplification, or duplicate work is removed.
2. Hot-cell benchmarks show the mode improved on its own terms across the same placement profiles used in H1.
3. The cleanup does not change the project's MVCC-by-default behavior.
4. Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
5. Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","notes":"This bead is likely to intersect both retry policy and commit-path scope. It should lean on B1's lock map rather than rediscovering the same synchronization topology from scratch, and it should be careful not to turn single-writer cleanup into a justification for weakening MVCC.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:18:06.438428589Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-15T09:28:44Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["busy","critical-path","db300-program","db300-track-h","legacy","locking","perf","sqlite","track-h"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.8.3","title":"H3: verify shared optimizations help single-writer mode and document its role","description":"## Goal
Verify that the shared hot-path optimizations from the WAL and execution-engine tracks are rerun in forced single-writer mode through the operator-friendly verification path, then document the intended long-term role of the mode in benchmark reporting and fallback behavior. This bead keeps the final story honest and prevents single-writer from becoming an afterthought or a misleading benchmark strawman.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Shared improvements from the major WAL and execution-engine beads are benchmarked in single-writer mode as well as MVCC on the canonical matrix and relevant placement profiles.
2. The verification uses the rich-log verification-suite entrypoint rather than ad hoc commands.
3. The final role of single-writer mode is documented clearly as comparison or fallback rather than product default, including any placement-profile caveats needed for honesty.
4. The bead produces inputs that G4 can use directly in the final report.
5. The resulting design or decision record includes an explicit verification plan describing the unit tests, end-to-end scenarios, and logging artifacts required for follow-on implementation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The resulting design or decision record includes an explicit verification plan describing the unit tests, end-to-end scenarios, and logging artifacts required for follow-on implementation.","notes":"This bead is about closure and honest positioning. In practice this means rerunning the canonical single-writer matrix after the shared WAL and execution-engine optimization tranches land, not sampling a single convenience benchmark and declaring victory.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:19:27.307774414Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:18.577279803Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-h","evidence","legacy","perf","report","sqlite","track-h"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:10Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -511,6 +511,27 @@ campaign manifest, but unless the operator also supplies external CPU and memory
 binding, the collector will mark them as declared-only rather than cleanly
 comparable topology-enforced runs.
 
+When `BEAD_ID=bd-db300.8.1.2`, the collector also emits:
+
+- `classification.json`
+- `classification.md`
+
+Those artifacts live beside the run-level `report.json` and `summary.md`. They
+combine the fresh declared-only H1.2 packs with the latest comparable
+`bd-db300.8.1.1` baseline report for `frankensqlite`, so H2 gets an explicit
+classification instead of only raw pack deltas.
+
+If you already have the pack manifests and only need to regenerate the
+classification or run-level report after script updates, reuse the existing
+output directory:
+
+```bash
+POSTPROCESS_ONLY=1 \
+OUTPUT_DIR=artifacts/perf/bd-db300.8.1.2/<run_id> \
+BEAD_ID=bd-db300.8.1.2 \
+bash scripts/verify_bd_db300_8_1_1_matched_artifact_packs.sh
+```
+
 ### Persistent Phase-Attribution Packs
 
 `bd-db300.1.7.2` uses the persistent Criterion harness, not `realdb-e2e bench`.

@@ -23,6 +23,8 @@ PACKS_DIR="${OUTPUT_DIR}/packs"
 LOG_FILE="${OUTPUT_DIR}/events.jsonl"
 REPORT_JSON="${OUTPUT_DIR}/report.json"
 SUMMARY_MD="${OUTPUT_DIR}/summary.md"
+CLASSIFICATION_JSON="${OUTPUT_DIR}/classification.json"
+CLASSIFICATION_MD="${OUTPUT_DIR}/classification.md"
 ROW_IDS="${ROW_IDS:-mixed_read_write_c4}"
 FIXTURE_IDS="${FIXTURE_IDS:-}"
 PLACEMENT_PROFILE_IDS="${PLACEMENT_PROFILE_IDS:-baseline_unpinned}"
@@ -32,6 +34,8 @@ WARMUP="${WARMUP:-0}"
 CARGO_PROFILE="${CARGO_PROFILE:-release-perf}"

 RETENTION_CLASS="${RETENTION_CLASS:-quick_run}"
+POSTPROCESS_ONLY="${POSTPROCESS_ONLY:-0}"
+EMIT_SINGLE_WRITER_CLASSIFICATION="${EMIT_SINGLE_WRITER_CLASSIFICATION:-0}"
 BEADS_DATA_PATH="${WORKSPACE_ROOT}/.beads/issues.jsonl"
 SOURCE_REVISION="${SOURCE_REVISION:-$(git -C "${WORKSPACE_ROOT}" rev-parse HEAD)}"
 BEADS_HASH="${BEADS_HASH:-$(sha256sum "${BEADS_DATA_PATH}" | awk '{print $1}')}"
@@ -84,6 +88,15 @@ short_hash() {
     printf '%s' "$1" | cut -c1-12
 }
 
+latest_report_file() {
+    local root="$1"
+    find "${root}" -type f -name report.json 2>/dev/null | sort | tail -n 1 || true
+}
+
+should_emit_single_writer_classification() {
+    [[ "${BEAD_ID}" == "bd-db300.8.1.2" || "${EMIT_SINGLE_WRITER_CLASSIFICATION}" == "1" ]]
+}
+
 ensure_row_exists() {
     local row_id="$1"
     jq -e --arg row_id "${row_id}" '.matrix_rows[] | select(.row_id == $row_id)' "${CAMPAIGN_MANIFEST_FILE}" >/dev/null \
@@ -619,35 +632,205 @@ build_report() {
     ' "${REPORT_JSON}" > "${SUMMARY_MD}"
 }
 
+build_single_writer_classification() {
+    local baseline_report_json="${BASELINE_REPORT_JSON:-$(latest_report_file "${WORKSPACE_ROOT}/artifacts/perf/bd-db300.8.1.1")}"
+    [[ -n "${baseline_report_json}" ]] || fail "classification" "failed to resolve a baseline bd-db300.8.1.1 report.json"
+    require_nonempty_file "${baseline_report_json}"
+    require_nonempty_file "${REPORT_JSON}"
+
+    jq -n \
+        --arg schema_version "fsqlite-e2e.db300.single_writer_classification.v1" \
+        --arg bead_id "${BEAD_ID}" \
+        --arg run_id "${RUN_ID}" \
+        --arg generated_at "${GENERATED_AT}" \
+        --arg baseline_report_json "${baseline_report_json}" \
+        --arg current_report_json "${REPORT_JSON}" \
+        --slurpfile baseline "${baseline_report_json}" \
+        --slurpfile current "${REPORT_JSON}" \
+        '
+        def pack($doc; $placement; $storage):
+            $doc[0].packs[]
+            | select(
+                .fixture_id == "frankensqlite"
+                and .placement_profile_id == $placement
+                and .storage_profile_id == $storage
+            );
+        (pack($baseline; "baseline_unpinned"; "file_backed")) as $baseline_file_backed |
+        (pack($baseline; "baseline_unpinned"; "memory")) as $baseline_memory |
+        (pack($current; "recommended_pinned"; "file_backed")) as $recommended_file_backed |
+        (pack($current; "recommended_pinned"; "memory")) as $recommended_memory |
+        (pack($current; "adversarial_cross_node"; "file_backed")) as $adversarial_file_backed |
+        (pack($current; "adversarial_cross_node"; "memory")) as $adversarial_memory |
+        {
+          schema_version: $schema_version,
+          bead_id: $bead_id,
+          run_id: $run_id,
+          generated_at: $generated_at,
+          current_report_json: $current_report_json,
+          baseline_report_json: $baseline_report_json,
+          evidence_scope: {
+            fixture_id: "frankensqlite",
+            workload_row: "mixed_read_write_c4",
+            comparable_baseline_pack: {
+              placement_profile_id: "baseline_unpinned",
+              report_json: $baseline_report_json
+            },
+            declared_only_follow_on_packs: [
+              {
+                placement_profile_id: "recommended_pinned",
+                report_json: $current_report_json
+              },
+              {
+                placement_profile_id: "adversarial_cross_node",
+                report_json: $current_report_json
+              }
+            ]
+          },
+          findings: [
+            {
+              category: "unavoidable_serialization",
+              status: "not_primary_explanation_for_the_extra_file_backed_gap",
+              confidence: "high",
+              evidence: {
+                baseline_memory_single_writer_vs_mvcc_ratio: $baseline_memory.deltas.single_writer_vs_mvcc_median_ops_ratio,
+                recommended_pinned_memory_single_writer_vs_mvcc_ratio: $recommended_memory.deltas.single_writer_vs_mvcc_median_ops_ratio,
+                adversarial_cross_node_memory_single_writer_vs_mvcc_ratio: $adversarial_memory.deltas.single_writer_vs_mvcc_median_ops_ratio,
+                baseline_memory_retry_delta: $baseline_memory.deltas.single_writer_minus_mvcc_mean_retries,
+                recommended_pinned_memory_retry_delta: $recommended_memory.deltas.single_writer_minus_mvcc_mean_retries,
+                adversarial_cross_node_memory_retry_delta: $adversarial_memory.deltas.single_writer_minus_mvcc_mean_retries
+              },
+              rationale: "When storage is memory-backed, single-writer no longer trails MVCC and retry deltas collapse to zero across the baseline and both declared placements, so serialization alone does not explain the extra file-backed slowdown.",
+              action_for_h2: "Prioritize the file-backed single-writer durability or queue path before trying broad shared-engine reductions."
+            },
+            {
+              category: "avoidable_wait_and_queue_amplification",
+              status: "confirmed",
+              confidence: "high",
+              evidence: {
+                baseline_file_backed_single_writer_vs_mvcc_ratio: $baseline_file_backed.deltas.single_writer_vs_mvcc_median_ops_ratio,
+                baseline_file_backed_retry_delta: $baseline_file_backed.deltas.single_writer_minus_mvcc_mean_retries,
+                adversarial_cross_node_file_backed_single_writer_vs_mvcc_ratio: $adversarial_file_backed.deltas.single_writer_vs_mvcc_median_ops_ratio,
+                adversarial_cross_node_file_backed_retry_delta: $adversarial_file_backed.deltas.single_writer_minus_mvcc_mean_retries,
+                recommended_pinned_file_backed_single_writer_vs_mvcc_ratio: $recommended_file_backed.deltas.single_writer_vs_mvcc_median_ops_ratio,
+                recommended_pinned_file_backed_retry_delta: $recommended_file_backed.deltas.single_writer_minus_mvcc_mean_retries
+              },
+              rationale: "The comparable baseline pack and the declared-only adversarial pack both show single-writer throughput collapsing below MVCC while retry deltas remain materially positive. Recommended-pinned reduces the damage, which points to queueing or wait amplification in the file-backed single-writer path rather than a fixed engine tax.",
+              action_for_h2: "Target the single-writer file-backed retry and baton-passing path first, then re-measure before touching broader shared decode or planner costs."
+            },
+            {
+              category: "topology_sensitivity",
+              status: "suggested_but_not_transportable_without_external_binding",
+              confidence: "mixed",
+              evidence: {
+                recommended_pinned_file_backed_single_writer_vs_mvcc_ratio: $recommended_file_backed.deltas.single_writer_vs_mvcc_median_ops_ratio,
+                adversarial_cross_node_file_backed_single_writer_vs_mvcc_ratio: $adversarial_file_backed.deltas.single_writer_vs_mvcc_median_ops_ratio,
+                recommended_pinned_comparability: $recommended_file_backed.comparability_status,
+                adversarial_cross_node_comparability: $adversarial_file_backed.comparability_status
+              },
+              rationale: "Declared-only placement variants separate into a healthier recommended-pinned file-backed pack and a much worse adversarial cross-node pack, so placement likely changes the severity of the wait amplification. The claim stays mixed because the collector does not enforce external CPU or NUMA binding.",
+              action_for_h2: "Prefer fixes that reduce queue ownership handoff sensitivity even before topology-enforced reruns exist."
+            },
+            {
+              category: "wake_storms",
+              status: "not_directly_measured_by_matched_pack",
+              confidence: "mixed",
+              evidence: {
+                supporting_signal: "retry and latency deltas imply extra waiting, but no explicit wake counter is captured in this artifact family"
+              },
+              rationale: "The matched packs prove waiting and retry amplification, but they do not attribute that waiting to wake storms versus other queue mechanisms.",
+              action_for_h2: "If the first queue-path fix does not collapse the gap, add explicit wake and handoff counters before deeper speculation."
+            },
+            {
+              category: "ownership_churn",
+              status: "not_supported_by_current_pack",
+              confidence: "mixed",
+              evidence: {
+                supporting_signal: "ownership churn is tracked by the shared D/J evidence lanes rather than this matched-pack collector"
+              },
+              rationale: "These matched packs isolate comparison-mode behavior, but they do not directly measure PageData ownership reuse or clone-heavy transitions.",
+              action_for_h2: "Treat ownership churn as a secondary hypothesis only after single-writer queue and retry fixes are tested."
+            },
+            {
+              category: "duplicate_work",
+              status: "not_supported_by_current_pack",
+              confidence: "mixed",
+              evidence: {
+                supporting_signal: "no subphase counters in this pack identify duplicated serialized work"
+              },
+              rationale: "The artifact family does not expose subphase duplication directly, so duplicate work remains a follow-on hypothesis instead of the leading explanation.",
+              action_for_h2: "Do not broaden H2 into speculative duplicate-work cleanup unless the targeted queue-path changes fail to move the file-backed cells."
+            }
+          ],
+          primary_h2_actions: [
+            "Fix file-backed single-writer retry and queue amplification before shared-engine cleanup.",
+            "Keep non-baseline placement evidence labeled declared-only until external placement enforcement exists.",
+            "Revisit wake storms, ownership churn, and duplicate work only if the targeted queue-path fix fails to remove the file-backed gap."
+          ]
+        }
+        ' > "${CLASSIFICATION_JSON}"
+
+    jq -r '
+        [
+            "# H1.2 Single-Writer Slowdown Classification",
+            "",
+            "- run_id: `\(.run_id)`",
+            "- baseline_report_json: `\(.baseline_report_json)`",
+            "- current_report_json: `\(.current_report_json)`",
+            "",
+            "## Findings",
+            "",
+            (
+                .findings[]
+                | "- `\(.category)`: `\(.status)` (\(.confidence))"
+            ),
+            "",
+            "## Actionable Direction For H2",
+            "",
+            (.primary_h2_actions[] | "- " + .)
+        ] | join("\n")
+    ' "${CLASSIFICATION_JSON}" > "${CLASSIFICATION_MD}"
+}
+
 main() {
     require_file "${CAMPAIGN_MANIFEST_FILE}"
     require_nonempty_file "${BEADS_DATA_PATH}"
-    log_event "INFO" "start" "starting matched artifact pack collection"
-
-    local row_id fixture_id placement_profile_id storage_profile_id
-    while IFS= read -r row_id; do

-        ensure_row_exists "${row_id}"
-        while IFS= read -r fixture_id; do

-            while IFS= read -r placement_profile_id; do

-                while IFS= read -r storage_profile_id; do

-                    validate_storage_profile "${storage_profile_id}"
-                    collect_pack "${row_id}" "${fixture_id}" "${placement_profile_id}" "${storage_profile_id}"
-                done < <(csv_to_lines "${STORAGE_PROFILE_IDS}")
-            done < <(row_placement_profiles "${row_id}")
-        done < <(row_fixture_ids "${row_id}")
-    done < <(csv_to_lines "${ROW_IDS}")
+    if [[ "${POSTPROCESS_ONLY}" == "1" ]]; then
+        log_event "INFO" "start" "postprocess-only matched artifact pack synthesis started"
+    else
+        log_event "INFO" "start" "starting matched artifact pack collection"
+
+        local row_id fixture_id placement_profile_id storage_profile_id
+        while IFS= read -r row_id; do
+            [[ -n "${row_id}" ]] || continue
+            ensure_row_exists "${row_id}"
+            while IFS= read -r fixture_id; do
+                [[ -n "${fixture_id}" ]] || continue
+                while IFS= read -r placement_profile_id; do
+                    [[ -n "${placement_profile_id}" ]] || continue
+                    while IFS= read -r storage_profile_id; do
+                        [[ -n "${storage_profile_id}" ]] || continue
+                        validate_storage_profile "${storage_profile_id}"
+                        collect_pack "${row_id}" "${fixture_id}" "${placement_profile_id}" "${storage_profile_id}"
+                    done < <(csv_to_lines "${STORAGE_PROFILE_IDS}")
+                done < <(row_placement_profiles "${row_id}")
+            done < <(row_fixture_ids "${row_id}")
+        done < <(csv_to_lines "${ROW_IDS}")
+    fi
 
     build_report
+    if should_emit_single_writer_classification; then
+        build_single_writer_classification
+    fi
     log_event "INFO" "complete" "matched artifact pack collection completed"
 
     echo "RUN_ID:      ${RUN_ID}"
     echo "OUTPUT_DIR:  ${OUTPUT_DIR}"
     echo "REPORT_JSON: ${REPORT_JSON}"
     echo "SUMMARY_MD:  ${SUMMARY_MD}"
+    if should_emit_single_writer_classification; then
+        echo "CLASSIFICATION_JSON: ${CLASSIFICATION_JSON}"
+        echo "CLASSIFICATION_MD:   ${CLASSIFICATION_MD}"
+    fi
 }
 
 main "$@"
